sentences.text: Hi, Jennifer.
Hi, sorry, my, Zoom decided to restart, right?
Right as I was logging in, and it, like, my whole computer went dark, so….
Totally, totally okay.
Apologies for my attire, I'm, actually traveling this week, so….
I saw your message.
So, you could have waited to do this, you didn't have to do it while you were….
Totally fine.
I saw, Backstreet Boys at the Sphere last night.
Oh, that's awesome.
So, very, very cool piece of nostalgia, but, … and then we're doing some hiking, so I'm just, just trying to wrap things up today, but….
Alright, yeah, let's get it wrapped up then.
Yeah.
You got your business?
Yeah, so tell me what's going on.
I think I know who's taking over the properties, because I was just at their conference in Phoenix a couple weeks ago.
Yeah, we've got a few, actually, so, ….
Okay.
Our parent company decided that they did not want to manage residential in-house anymore, so what we've done is we've contracted with Cushman & Wakefield, and Rosen Associates are the two main ones.
To take over the day-to-day, like, management operation of our residential property.
So, Cushman has the majority of them, and then Rose has everything that was in New York City, basically.
Okay.

And so what I'm trying to do is still… I would still like to have access to the product, because I love it, and I want to be able to check market data and information, and I know previously, our MAC property management account was set up as a per unit.
That we were billing back to the properties.
Yep.
And so I wanted to… and I could not find my notes from my previous conversation, but I knew that you guys had.
the two different billing, options.
per user or per unit.
Yeah.
And so, since the properties won't cover my access anymore, I wanted to find out what that per user was, so that I could still maintain access.
Yeah, so the good news is we work with both Rose and Cushman.
A different set of Rose is, like, they have every property that they're managing on the Farm MyQ.
With Cushman, to this point, it's been an order form process, so an MSA was signed.
I think July of last year, and we've onboarded about, like, 250 properties with it.
It's up to the ownership group to decide to do that or not.
We've heard that it's going to be a mandated software for them next year.
So, two ways you could go about this.
You could get read-only access through Cushman.
Meaning, you could get a login just to see all of your sites, if that's where the interest level is.

from the, … and the way that that would work is, I don't know who your, regional is, or who the client services person is there, but they would just have to fill out the order forms for each of those properties, and we'd onboard them, and then get you a seat.
The other way to do this is what you said, which is, like a user license option.
And that includes other components of DepartmentIQ, namely the research component, if you're using that.
Yeah, that's what I'm looking for, actually.
Got it.
Okay, so let's talk about that, then.
And is it just yourself that's gonna need a seat?
I think for now, I can find out if any of our asset managers would want to see, just depending on cost, but, they typically use, like, CoStar and other things, which I don't… I don't prefer.
Okay.
So, I think it would be me for now, but potentially a few additional people.
Okay, the reason I'm asking is it's kind of like, there's brackets.
So the first bracket is up to 5 seats, 15,000 annually.
Now, because you have the Cushman tie-in, I can try and work with finance and get that reduced, if it's just gonna be yourself.
One of the reasons I want to connect on a call, though, is we did just release, essentially, like, functionally a CoStar competitor.
Okay.
So, you were using the research section, which is, like, you're just creating the same set of data you're getting for your properties.
Did you ever use, like, the explore section of the tool?
Did you have access to that?
I don't think we had access to that.
Okay, let me quickly show you what that looks like.
And then I'd be happy to set up time with a few of the asset managers to make sure, like, in giving you an account, it's worth the… Budget that you'd be paying?
Alright…
You'll see my screen.
Yes.
Okay, so you'll see here on the left-hand side, this is the full product suite now.
We are releasing a revenue management tool, as well.
Yeah, I actually asked Cushman to look into that, because I'm.
Yeah, so, I spoke with Nathan and Sandra at the leadership conference.

They have a pretty insane, like, governance process, and so it'd be a while before I think we're able to get there, but we are talking with them about potentially becoming approved software for them in that space.
Explore here is… there's kind of two components of it.
So you mentioned, like, New York, or we pull up Phoenix.
Functionally, what you have here is that IQ, pricing and availability data in inventory.
Okay.
And then, it also has new supply data and demographic information.
And then we've partnered with a third-party data provider, to backfill things like ownership or transaction information to get it closer to CoStar.
Okay.
So, that's what this looks like.
You have existing inventory over here.
New supply data here, market summary, 30-day snapshot.
Historical trend data here.
Oh, perfect, okay.
And then any of these components can be clicked into.
For more information, so you've got rental performance, again, 30-day snapshot.
Here's your new supply.
demographic information, etc.
And you can drill into these as much as you want.
And then, kind of similar UI to CoStar here, where each property has a property page.
And then when you click into that, this is a bad example, actually.
So, get some filters on.
You're able to kind of filter down to the…
If I just take… let's just take Cushman, for example, here, actually.
And if I want to look into this property here… So, you're gonna have your building summary, your performance trends.
Floor plan and availability.
So, all that, like, information that you're accessing and research, you can do directly out of this section now, including generating that kind of, like, underwriting support, report.
Okay.
And then, if you're using anything like a Market Analytics, this, market comparison tool.
Is coming soon as well.
And that is just, all the data points in terms of, like, performance.
All your data points on, supply and inventory.
Unit delivery, transaction sizes, all your trend data on those markets.
Nope.
And then all your demographic information.
So if you're tracking 10 different markets for acquisitions, you can see how those are performing, over time, and you can select which metrics you're looking at.
You can also filter into, like, the type of Property that you'd typically make an acquisition on, as well.
Okay, understood.
So, that was, like, the highest, highest level version of that, but, … that Explore tool would be included in the 5-seat arrangement as well.
Okay.
Okay.
Let me, … if you can check on the single seat and see if that's even an option.

If it's not, we… we definitely would not have more than five that would want access to that, so if it is the… if the 5-seat… is the minimum, then, I just need to find out if we're still able to bill it back through, like, ownership costs, so, because I don't have, like, a P&L anymore, so… so, I can find out that information in the mean… in the meantime.
And then, worst case, you know, we can go the read-only access route for now.
Yeah.
Via Cushman.
I would have to probably do it through Cushman and Rose, right, to see the properties?
Yeah, and I think Rose, like, onboards everything, so they should… they should have added these properties in, so you can access that.
Cushman's just like, you just gotta get the order forms filled out, and then… it's, 45 cents a unit is the rate that they've negotiated.
Okay, but I would… I would have to basically log in to two different environments, right, to see the properties.
I wouldn't be able to see them in one.
Correct, yeah.
Nope.
So, and I've seen, like, 3-seat arrangements, we just did one with KKR, actually, like, in a pilot scenario, so I'll work with finance and see what we can do.
Okay.
Let me do that, and then follow up with you.
Okay, perfect.
Awesome.
Quick and easy.
Thank you so much?
Great seeing you.
Thanks, Matthew, have fun.
Alright, bye-bye.
